Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Kane, Esq. (415-982-1510), for David Tse, Plaintiff.
Eamon Jafari, Esq. of Barrington Legal, (310-231-8755), for Freedom West Homes Corporation, Defendant.

Hearing is held as to the status quo of the meetings on July 18, July 19, July 28 and August 3, and the notice sent re August 3 meeting. Plaintiff argues that there was proper quorom on July 28 election and the July 19 meeting did not happen.

Defendant argues that August 3 meeting was to remove the Directors. Plaintiff argues that the August 3 meeting to remove Directors was not properly noticed and believes that Defendant wanted to discertify the election held on July 18.

Arguments on the adjournment issue re the July 28 meeting and the validity of the elction is held.

Defendant adds that granting preliminary injunction will result to irreparable damage to the community based on David Tse's action and behavior as to manipulation of selling properties to developers.

Plaintiff requests the Court to require the minimum bond posting if the preliminary injunction is granted. Defendant requests the Court to require $200,000 bond posting.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments rules as follows: Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction is TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
